title: Efficient delivery of microorganisms produced from vermicomposting to soil
abstract: A process for the preparation of a soil inoculating composition comprising the steps of a) loading a vermicompost into a tank, b) adding a sufficient amount of water and molasses to vermicompost, c) breeding of microorganism in the mixture obtained in the step b), by adjusting the temperature to a range between 25-27Â°C and by providing air into the mixture, d) forcing microorganism in the mixture obtained in the step c), to dormant state by d) adding a sufficient amount of molasses, e) bottling the composition obtained in the step d), for storage, f) resuscitating of microorganism in the stored composition from the dormant state to active state by diluting the composition with a sufficient amount of water before soil inoculation. An apparatus for producing soil inoculating composition comprising two concentric tanks wherein the inner tanks (104) holds the composition and the outer tank (101) acting as an heating system holds a liquid kept at a desired temperature by an heat control system (106).
claims: Claims 1. A process for the preparation of a soil inoculating composition comprising the steps of a) loading a vermicompost into a tank, b) adding a sufficient amount of water and molasses to vermicompost, c) breeding of microorganism in the mixture obtained in the step b), by adjusting the temperature to a range between 25Î and 27 < C and by providing air into the mixture, d) forcing microorganism in the mixture obtained in the step c), to dormant state by d) adding a sufficient amount of molasses, e) bottling the composition obtained in the step d), for storage, f) resuscitating of microorganism in the stored composition from the dormant state to active state by diluting the composition with a sufficient amount of water before soil inoculation. 2. The process according to claim 1 , wherein the ratio of vermicompost to water added in step b) is in the range of between 1 :1 and 1 :3. 3. The process according to claim 2, wherein ratio of vermicompost to water is 1 :1.5. 4. The process according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein water is non- chlorinated water. 5. The process according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the ratio of the mixture of vermicompost and water to molasses is in the range of between 15:1 and 5:1. 6. The process according to claim 5, wherein the ratio of the mixture of vermicompost and water to molasses is 10:1. 7. The process according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the ratio of molasses added in step d) to the mixture obtained from the step c) is in a ratio of between 1 :5 and 1 :15. 8. The process according to claim 7, wherein the ratio is 1 :9. 9. The process according to claim 1 , wherein the breeding step takes 6 to 10 hours. 10. The process according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the molasses is sugar cane molasses. 11. The process according to claim 1 , wherein the ratio of water is added in step f) to the stored composition for resuscitation of microorganism is in the range of between 1 :5 and 1 :15. 12. The process according to claim 11 , wherein the ratio of water added in step f) to the stored composition for resuscitation of microorganism is 1 :9. 13. The process according to claim 1 , wherein the resuscitation step takes 6 to 8 hours. 14. The process according to any one of the preceding claims, further comprising diluting the composition obtained in step f) with a sufficient amount of water. 15. The process according to claim 14, wherein the ratio of water to the composition is in the range of between 1 :5 and 1 :15. 16. The process according to claim 15, wherein the ratio of water to the composition is 1 :9. 17. An apparatus for producing soil inoculating composition comprising two concentric tanks wherein the inner tanks (104) holds the composition and the outer tank (101 ) acting as an heating system holds a liquid kept at a desired temperature by an heat control system (106). 18. The apparatus for producing soil inoculating composition according to claim 17, wherein said concentric tanks is cylindrical, conic or rectangular but identical wherein the outer tank is larger than the inner tank in such a manner that the outer tank covers the inner tank. 19. The apparatus for producing soil inoculating composition according to claim 17, wherein the liquid kept at a desired temperature in the outer tank is oil. 20. The apparatus for producing soil inoculating composition according to any one of claims 17 to 19, wherein heat control system (106) comprises a thermocouple (131 ), a heater (135), a heating metal plate (137) and a controller (133). 21. The apparatus for producing soil inoculating composition according to claim 20, wherein the controller (133) is Proportional-Integrated- Derivative (PID) controller which is an electronic component deployed to provide temperature feedback control from sensor (131 ) to heating plate (137) to eliminate the difference by heating or cooling the control variable, which is the liquid temperature. 22. The apparatus for producing soil inoculating composition according to claim 17, further comprising an aeration system (123) and an agitation system (105). 23. The apparatus for producing soil inoculating composition according to claim 22, wherein the aeration system is located under the tank providing air (129) into the mixture from the bottom of the tank. 24. The apparatus for producing soil inoculating composition according to claim 22, wherein the agitation system is a motor-operated rotational blade system mounted at the bottom of the tank wherein the system rotates to break up soil and worm casting into smaller pieces.
